skippedDuplicateDetail.reason: ORD-055 already authors this exact scenario (single-day inclusive boundary, Start=D AND End=D, monotonic-count assertion). Adding a new case would be semantic duplication. Gap consumed without generating a case.

ORD-054 authors this scenario in full: deliberately inverted range, [COND:] branches for validation-error vs auto-swap, no-crash assertion, network inspection. Adding a new case would be semantic duplication. Gap consumed without generating a case.

knownFailingReason: A1 anomaly: Custom Date End boundary is silently dropped from the GraphQL GetOrders filter on Apply. ORD-062 asserts the GQL filter contains both bounds — it will FAIL until A1 is fixed. ORD-069 asserts the End input is populated on panel reopen — it will FAIL due to A1/A2 (End dropped at apply-time). ORD-076 and ORD-077 test a11y anomalies A6 and A5 respectively; those are likely to fail but are classified 'Generated' (not 'knownFailing') because the behavior may have changed since exploration.
proposedBLFollowUp: A dedicated invariant BL-ORD-010 ('Order list query behavior: filtering, sorting, pagination, and scope isolation produce results consistent with the applied parameters') should be proposed to the business-logic custodian before the next sprint planning. DO NOT add this ID to any CSV row until formally approved. Current interim citation across this batch is BL-ORD-001.
notes: All 18 cases use live-discover patterns (no hardcoded order numbers, dates, or counts). Preset cases ORD-063–066 cite ECL-3.2 (filter persistence semantics). The four preset cases and ORD-062 are the highest-value outputs of this batch — they directly probe the A1 bug by asserting the GQL filter string shape. ORD-076/077 test a11y anomalies A5 and A6 from the exploration; observed failures in those cases should be filed as separate low-severity bugs.
